([Hungarian](/wiki/Hungarian_language "Hungarian language"): *Országgyűlés*, [lit.](/wiki/Literal_translation "Literal translation") 'Country Assembly' [[ˈorsaːɡɟyːleːʃ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Hungarian "Help:IPA/Hungarian")) is the [parliament](/wiki/Parliament "Parliament") of [Hungary](/wiki/Hungary "Hungary"). The unicameral body consists of 199 (386 between 1990 and 2014) members elected to four-year terms. Election of members is done using a [semi-proportional representation](/wiki/Semi-proportional_representation "Semi-proportional representation"): a [mixed-member majoritarian representation](/wiki/Mixed-member_majoritarian_representation "Mixed-member majoritarian representation") with partial [compensation via transfer votes](/wiki/Scorporo "Scorporo") and [mixed single vote](/wiki/Mixed_single_vote "Mixed single vote"); involving single-member districts and one list vote; parties must win at least 5% of the popular vote in order to gain list seats. The **National Assembly** ([Hungarian](/wiki/Hungarian_language "Hungarian language"): *Országgyűlés*, [lit.](/wiki/Literal_translation "Literal translation") 'Country Assembly' [[ˈorsaːɡɟyːleːʃ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Hungarian "Help:IPA/Hungarian")) is the [parliament](/wiki/Parliament "Parliament") of [Hungary](/wiki/Hungary "Hungary"). The unicameral body consists of 199 (386 between 1990 and 2014) members elected to four-year terms. Election of members is done using a [semi-proportional representation](/wiki/Semi-proportional_representation "Semi-proportional representation"): a [mixed-member majoritarian representation](/wiki/Mixed-member_majoritarian_representation "Mixed-member majoritarian representation") with partial [compensation via transfer votes](/wiki/Scorporo "Scorporo") and [mixed single vote](/wiki/Mixed_single_vote "Mixed single vote"); involving single-member districts and one list vote; parties must win at least 5% of the popular vote in order to gain list seats. The Assembly includes 25 standing committees to debate and report on introduced bills and to supervise the activities of the ministers. The [Constitutional Court of Hungary](/wiki/Constitutional_Court_of_Hungary "Constitutional Court of Hungary") has the right to challenge legislation on the grounds of constitutionality.

Under [communist rule](/wiki/Hungarian_People%27s_Republic "Hungarian People's Republic"), the National Assembly existed as the [supreme organ of state power](/wiki/Highest_organ_of_state_power "Highest organ of state power") as the sole branch of government in Hungary, and per the principle of [unified power](/wiki/Unified_power "Unified power"), all state organs were subservient to it. Since 1902, the assembly has met in the [Hungarian Parliament Building](/wiki/Hungarian_Parliament_Building "Hungarian Parliament Building") in [Budapest](/wiki/Budapest "Budapest").

The current members are the [members of the National Assembly of Hungary (2022–2026)](/wiki/List_of_members_of_the_National_Assembly_of_Hungary_(2022%E2%80%932026) "List of members of the National Assembly of Hungary (2022–2026)").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Assembly_(Hungary)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The [Diet of Hungary](/wiki/Diet_of_Hungary "Diet of Hungary")[[1]](#cite_note-5) ([Hungarian](/wiki/Hungarian_language "Hungarian language"): *Országgyűlés*) was a legislative institution in the [medieval kingdom of Hungary](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Hungary_in_the_Middle_Ages "Kingdom of Hungary in the Middle Ages") from the 1290s,[[2]](#cite_note-6) and in its successor states, [Royal Hungary](/wiki/Royal_Hungary "Royal Hungary") and the [Habsburg kingdom of Hungary](/wiki/Lands_of_the_Crown_of_Saint_Stephen "Lands of the Crown of Saint Stephen") throughout the [Early Modern period](/wiki/Early_Modern_period "Early Modern period"). The name of the legislative body was originally "Parlamentum" during the Middle Ages, the "Diet" expression gained mostly in the Early Modern period.[[3]](#cite_note-7) It convened at regular intervals with interruptions during the period of 1527 to 1918, and again until 1946.

In 1608, a [bicameral](/wiki/Bicameral "Bicameral") legislature was enacted as the Royal Hungarian Diet, dividing the main board and the lower board (the board of envoys). Members of the main board (the upper house) were the high nobles and high priests (archbishops and bishops). The lower board was attended by representatives of the common nobility, clergy and civil order: elected representatives of the noble county, delegates of the free royal cities and representatives of the lower Church representatives.[[4]](#cite_note-8)

Approximately 10% of the total voting age population could vote for the elected delegates of the lower board (5% county nobility, 5% residents of free royal cities). The election of the noble delegates (1 delegate from each county) took place in the county delegate elections, after a long, noisy, courtier campaign, at the county hall. Delegates received voting instructions from county assemblies.

The parliament consisted of about 500 people in the 17th–18th centuries.

The articles of the 1790 diet set out that the diet should meet at least once every 3 years, but, since the diet was called by the [Habsburg monarchy](/wiki/Habsburg_monarchy "Habsburg monarchy"), this promise was not kept on several occasions thereafter. As a result of the [Austro-Hungarian Compromise](/wiki/Austro-Hungarian_Compromise_of_1867 "Austro-Hungarian Compromise of 1867"), it was reconstituted in 1867.

The Latin term *Natio Hungarica* ("Hungarian nation") was used to designate the political elite which had participation in the diet, consisting of the [nobility](/wiki/Hungarian_nobility "Hungarian nobility"), the Catholic clergy, and a few enfranchised burghers,[[5]](#cite_note-9)[[6]](#cite_note-10) regardless of language or ethnicity.[[7]](#cite_note-11)

The democratic character of the Hungarian parliament was reestablished with [the fall of the Iron Curtain](/wiki/Collapse_of_communism "Collapse of communism") and the [end](/wiki/End_of_communism_in_Hungary_(1989) "End of communism in Hungary (1989)") of the [communist dictatorship](/wiki/Hungarian_People%27s_Republic "Hungarian People's Republic") in 1989. Today's parliament is still called the *Országgyűlés,* as in royal times, but is called the 'National Assembly' to distance itself from the historical royal diet.
